mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently predicts positive equity returns as corporate earnings track economic expansion, with higher growth rates directly translating to higher forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 1-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points compresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate hike signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate signals a labor market cooling that precedes earnings recessions, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns dominate when PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth directly translates to corporate revenue growth in cyclical sectors, boosting equity returns specifically during post-shock recovery phases when capacity utilization normalizes.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for equity returns during recovery phases, or forward returns are negative when INDPRO expands.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarter-over-quarter contraction in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals collapsing consumer confidence and credit conditions, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline does not predict negative 5-day forward returns, with equity returns remaining flat or positive.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated while the federal funds rate lags behind, real rates turn negative, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns do not materialize when CPIAUCSL is high and FEDFUNDS is relatively low, or real rates rise instead of falling.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer spending directly support corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the monthly horizon regardless of the broader business cycle phase.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward equity returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate triggers recession fears that override monetary easing benefits, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter specifically during early recession phases.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral despite sustained unemployment increases, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during confirmed recessions.
